title: US and Iran Reach Historic Peace Agreement to End Conflict
subtitle: International leaders welcome a new deal to stop military operations and reopen the vital Strait of Hormuz.

summary.en: The United States and Iran have officially reached a peace agreement to end their three-month conflict. Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif announced the deal, which mandates an immediate stop to military operations on all fronts. President Donald Trump confirmed the agreement and ordered the end of a naval blockade in the Strait of Hormuz. International leaders from Europe and the Middle East have expressed strong support for the breakthrough. A formal signing ceremony is scheduled to take place in Switzerland on June 19.
summary.zh: 美國與伊朗已正式達成和平協議，以終結雙方為期三個月的衝突。巴基斯坦總理夏巴茲·夏立夫宣布了這項協議，規定立即停止所有戰線上的軍事行動。川普總統證實了該協議，並下令解除荷莫茲海峽的軍事封鎖。來自歐洲與中東的國際領袖對此突破表達了強烈支持。正式簽署儀式預計於六月十九日在瑞士舉行。

paragraphs.en: The United States and Iran have reached a historic peace agreement, marking a major turning point after three months of intense conflict. The announcement was first made on Sunday by Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif, who played a key role in mediating the talks alongside Qatar. According to Sharif, the agreement brings an immediate and permanent end to all military operations, including those in Lebanon.
paragraphs.zh: 美國與伊朗達成了一項歷史性的和平協議，這標誌著雙方在經歷三個月的激烈衝突後，出現了重大的轉折點。這項消息最初由巴基斯坦總理夏巴茲·夏立夫（Shehbaz Sharif）於週日宣布，他在促成此次談判的過程中，與卡達共同扮演了關鍵的調解角色。根據夏立夫的說法，該協議將立即且永久地終止所有軍事行動，包括在黎巴嫩境內的行動。

paragraphs.en: United States President Donald Trump confirmed the news on his social media platform, stating that the deal with the Islamic Republic of Iran is now complete. As part of the agreement, President Trump announced that he has authorized the removal of the United States naval blockade in the Strait of Hormuz. This narrow waterway is a critical route for global oil shipments. By reopening the strait, the president said he hopes to allow oil to flow freely again for the benefit of the entire world. The opening is expected to begin officially after the formal signing of the agreement, which is scheduled for June 19 in Switzerland.
paragraphs.zh: 美國總統川普（Donald Trump）在社群媒體平台上證實了這項消息，並表示與伊朗伊斯蘭共和國達成的協議現已完成。作為協議的一部分，川普總統宣布他已授權解除美國在荷莫茲海峽的軍事封鎖。這條狹窄的水道是全球石油運輸的關鍵航線。總統表示，透過重新開放該海峽，他希望讓石油能再次自由流通，以造福全世界。預計在六月十九日於瑞士正式簽署協議後，該海峽將正式開放。

paragraphs.en: Iranian officials have also confirmed the deal. Kazem Gharibabadi, Iran’s deputy foreign minister, stated in televised comments that the agreement creates a permanent end to the war on all fronts. While the exact details of the memorandum of understanding remain under review, Iranian state media reported that the plan includes reopening the Strait of Hormuz for a period of 30 days under specific Iranian arrangements. This step is intended to allow for the removal of underwater mines that were placed during the recent period of fighting.
paragraphs.zh: 伊朗官員也證實了這項協議。伊朗外交部副部長加里巴巴迪（Kazem Gharibabadi）在電視評論中表示，該協議在所有戰線上永久終止了戰爭。雖然諒解備忘錄的確切細節仍在審查中，但伊朗官媒報導稱，該計畫包括在伊朗特定的安排下，重新開放荷莫茲海峽三十天。此舉旨在清除近期戰鬥期間所布設的水雷。

paragraphs.en: The path to this peace deal was not easy. Negotiations were nearly derailed earlier in the weekend when an Israeli airstrike hit the southern suburbs of Beirut. According to The Guardian, the strike destroyed a building and caused several casualties, leading to strong warnings from Iranian officials. President Trump later told the media that the attack had delayed the finalization of the deal by a few hours. Despite these tensions, the mediation efforts led by Pakistan and Qatar successfully brought both sides to an agreement.
paragraphs.zh: 通往這項和平協議的道路並不容易。週末早些時候，當以色列的空襲擊中貝魯特南部郊區時，談判幾乎陷入停滯。根據《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，這次空襲摧毀了一棟建築並造成數人傷亡，引發伊朗官員的強烈警告。川普總統隨後向媒體表示，這次攻擊使協議的最終定案延遲了幾個小時。儘管存在這些緊張局勢，由巴基斯坦和卡達主導的調解工作仍成功促使雙方達成協議。

paragraphs.en: International leaders have responded to the announcement with widespread relief and optimism. In a joint statement, the leaders of Germany, the United Kingdom, France, and Italy welcomed the breakthrough. British Prime Minister Keir Starmer described the agreement as a hugely important step forward in ending the war. He emphasized that the international community must now focus on ensuring the Strait of Hormuz remains open to help the global economy. Furthermore, Starmer noted that these European nations are prepared to lift certain sanctions—official punishments that limit trade—against Iran, provided that the country takes clear and verifiable steps regarding its nuclear program.
paragraphs.zh: 國際領袖對此宣布表示廣泛的寬慰與樂觀。德國、英國、法國和義大利的領袖發表聯合聲明，對此突破表示歡迎。英國首相施凱爾（Keir Starmer）形容該協議是終結戰爭極為重要的一步。他強調，國際社會現在必須專注於確保荷莫茲海峽保持暢通，以協助全球經濟。此外，施凱爾指出，只要伊朗針對其核子計畫採取明確且可驗證的步驟，這些歐洲國家準備解除對伊朗的部分制裁，即限制貿易的官方懲罰措施。

paragraphs.en: Qatar, which served as a primary mediator, also expressed its support. The Qatari prime minister and foreign minister, Mohammed bin Abdulrahman Al Thani, stated that they look forward to all parties working together in a positive spirit to build upon this progress. Pakistan’s Prime Minister Sharif also thanked the leadership of Saudi Arabia and Turkey for their contributions to the diplomatic process, noting that the agreement provides a foundation for future technical talks.
paragraphs.zh: 擔任主要調解方的卡達也表達了支持。卡達總理兼外交部長穆罕默德（Mohammed bin Abdulrahman Al Thani）表示，他們期待所有相關方能以積極的精神共同努力，在此進展的基礎上再接再厲。巴基斯坦總理夏立夫也感謝沙烏地阿拉伯與土耳其的領導層對外交進程的貢獻，並指出該協議為未來的技術性會談奠定了基礎。

paragraphs.en: For many, the deal represents a rare moment of success in a region that has faced significant instability for months. President Trump suggested that this agreement could bring long-term peace and security to the area, noting that many previous leaders had tried and failed to reach such an understanding. As the world watches, the focus now shifts to the upcoming meetings in Switzerland. These discussions are expected to finalize the technical details of the peace deal and ensure that the ceasefire—an agreement to stop fighting—is respected by all sides involved. With the naval blockade ending and military operations halting, the international community hopes that this agreement will mark the beginning of a more stable period for the region.
paragraphs.zh: 對許多人而言，這項協議代表了該地區在經歷數月嚴重不穩定後，難得一見的成功時刻。川普總統認為，這項協議可能為該地區帶來長期的和平與安全，並指出許多前任領導人都曾嘗試達成此類共識但以失敗告終。隨著全球關注，焦點現在轉向即將在瑞士舉行的會議。這些討論預計將敲定和平協議的技術細節，並確保停火協議，即停止戰鬥的共識，能受到所有相關方的尊重。隨著軍事封鎖結束與軍事行動停止，國際社會希望這項協議能標誌著該地區進入一個更穩定期。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To cause a plan or process to fail or be interrupted.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 使（計畫、進程）失敗或中斷；原意指火車出軌。
keyTerms.noteZh: 原意指火車出軌，這裡比喻計畫受阻。文中：Negotiations were nearly derailed earlier in the weekend when an Israeli airstrike hit the southern suburbs of Beirut.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To officially end a law, rule, or restriction.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（電梯）或動詞（舉起），這裡指解除制裁。文中：Furthermore, Starmer noted that these European nations are prepared to lift certain sanctions—official punishments that limit trade—against Iran, provided that the country takes clear and verifiable steps regarding its nuclear program.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To use an achievement or success as a base for further development.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 在……的基礎上進一步發展。
keyTerms.noteZh: 由動詞 build 和介詞 upon 組成，意指利用現有成果繼續努力。文中：The Qatari prime minister and foreign minister, Mohammed bin Abdulrahman Al Thani, stated that they look forward to all parties working together in a positive spirit to build upon this progress.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To move the focus or attention from one thing to another.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（輪班），這裡作動詞指焦點轉移。文中：As the world watches, the focus now shifts to the upcoming meetings in Switzerland.

sources.name: Deutsche Welle (DW) Top
sources.title: Iran war: Pakistan announces US-Iran peace deal reached

sources.name: The Guardian World
sources.title: Peace deal between US and Iran, with strait of Hormuz to reopen

sources.name: Al Jazeera English
sources.title: US-Iran ceasefire deal announced, Trump says Strait of Hormuz reopening

previousArticles.title: US and Iran Reach Peace Deal to End Conflict
previousArticles.summaryEn: The United States and Iran have officially reached a peace agreement to end their three-month conflict. Pakistan’s Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif announced the deal, which includes a permanent stop to military operations. President Donald Trump confirmed the agreement and ordered an end to the US naval blockade in the Strait of Hormuz. Despite recent tensions caused by Israeli airstrikes in Lebanon, both sides are moving toward a formal signing ceremony. The agreement is expected to restore global shipping routes and address concerns regarding nuclear materials.

previousArticles.title: US and Iran Move Closer to Potential Peace Agreement
previousArticles.summaryEn: President Donald Trump claims a peace deal with Iran could be signed as early as Sunday. The agreement aims to stop Iran from developing nuclear weapons and reopen the vital Strait of Hormuz. While some international leaders are optimistic, Iranian officials have suggested that the timeline is not yet certain. Both sides are currently trying to present the potential agreement as a victory for their own country. The situation remains tense as both nations continue to balance diplomatic talks with military actions.

previousArticles.title: US and Iran Near Potential Peace Agreement After Weeks of Conflict
previousArticles.summaryEn: President Donald Trump claims a peace deal with Iran is close to being signed. The agreement aims to stop Iran from building nuclear weapons and reopen the important Strait of Hormuz. While some international leaders are optimistic about a quick resolution, Iranian officials have asked for more patience. Recent days have seen both threats of military action and signs of diplomatic progress. The situation remains uncertain as both sides try to frame the potential agreement as a victory.

quiz.question: What is the primary reason for the temporary reopening of the Strait of Hormuz according to Iranian state media?

options.A: To allow the immediate resumption of global oil exports.
options.B: To facilitate the clearing of underwater mines placed during the conflict.
options.C: To fulfill the requirements set by the United States naval forces.
options.D: To demonstrate Iran's commitment to the upcoming Switzerland summit.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，伊朗官方媒體指出重新開放荷姆茲海峽是為了在特定安排下，清理戰鬥期間佈下的水雷。選項 A 是美國總統的願景，而非伊朗媒體所述的具體原因。選項 C 錯誤，因為這是雙方協議的一部分，而非單純滿足美軍要求。選項 D 與文章內容無關，文中並未將此舉與瑞士高峰會的展現掛鉤。

quiz.question: Based on the text, what can be inferred about the role of the Israeli airstrike in Beirut?

options.A: It permanently ended the possibility of a peace agreement.
options.B: It was coordinated with Pakistan to pressure Iranian negotiators.
options.C: It created a temporary obstacle that threatened to disrupt the ongoing mediation process.
options.D: It was the primary factor that convinced the United States to seek a deal.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第四段，空襲發生時談判幾乎「derailed」（脫軌/失敗），且導致伊朗發出強烈警告，川普也提到這延誤了協議最終化，這顯示空襲對談判造成了威脅。選項 A 錯誤，因為協議最終仍達成。選項 B 文中未提及以色列與巴基斯坦有任何協調。選項 D 錯誤，文中並未說明空襲是美國尋求協議的原因，僅提到它是一個阻礙談判進程的突發事件。

quiz.question: In the fifth paragraph, what does the word 'sanctions' refer to?

options.A: Official punishments that restrict trade and economic activity.
options.B: Military operations conducted to enforce a naval blockade.
options.C: Diplomatic rewards given for successful mediation efforts.
options.D: Technical agreements regarding nuclear safety standards.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。文章第五段明確定義了 sanctions 為「official punishments that limit trade」，即限制貿易的官方懲罰。選項 B 錯誤，制裁是經濟手段而非軍事行動。選項 C 錯誤，制裁是懲罰而非獎勵。選項 D 錯誤，雖然制裁與核計畫有關，但制裁本身是指懲罰措施，而非技術標準協議。

quiz.question: What is the central message of the article?

options.A: The United States and Iran have successfully resolved all their long-standing political differences.
options.B: Pakistan and Qatar are now the primary superpowers in the Middle East region.
options.C: A significant peace agreement has been reached to end the conflict between the US and Iran, offering hope for regional stability.
options.D: The Strait of Hormuz will remain closed until the final signing ceremony in Switzerland.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。全文主要報導美國與伊朗在巴基斯坦與卡達的斡旋下達成和平協議，並強調這對區域穩定與全球經濟的正面意義。選項 A 過於誇大，文中僅提到結束軍事衝突，未說解決所有差異。選項 B 無中生有，文中僅提到兩國作為斡旋者。選項 D 錯誤，文中提到 Strait of Hormuz 將會重新開放，而非保持關閉。
